allow: Bash(curl -s -X POST https://metalens-ai.vercel.app/api/synthesize -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "{""prompt"": ""You are a medical research analyst. Synthesize these PubMed abstracts into exactly 5 key findings.\\n\\nFormat each finding as:\\n**N. Short Title** — One concise conclusion sentence with specific data. Cite relevant PMIDs.\\n\\nCover: 1\) main comparison, 2\) statistics, 3\) recommendation, 4\) exceptions, 5\) limitations.\\n\\nRules: base claims ONLY on abstracts, cite PMIDs, use hedging language.\\n\\n--- ABSTRACTS ---\\n\\nPMID: 12699401 | Pranlukast: a review of its use in the management of asthma | Drugs \(2003\) | Pranlukast is an orally administered selective competitive antagonist of cysteinyl leukotrienes indicated for prophylactic treatment of chronic bronchial asthma in paediatric and adult patients.\\n\\nPMID: 26385352 | Antileukotrienes in upper airway inflammatory diseases | Current allergy and asthma reports \(2016\) | Leukotrienes are inflammatory mediators. By competitive binding to CysLT1 receptor, LT receptor antagonist drugs such as montelukast zafirlukast and pranlukast block inflammatory effects.\\n\\nPMID: 11336598 | Antileukotrienes in asthma: present situation | Expert opinion on pharmacotherapy \(2002\) | Over the last 5 years several antileukotrienes including montelukast pranlukast and zafirlukast have been marketed.\\n\\nPMID: 28301050 | Addition of anti-leukotriene agents to inhaled corticosteroids for adults and adolescents with persistent asthma | Cochrane \(2017\) | Guidelines recommend low-dose ICS as first-line therapy. Addition of anti-leukotriene agents to ICS offers a therapeutic option in suboptimal control.\\n\\nPMID: 15674901 | Long-acting beta2-agonists versus anti-leukotrienes as add-on therapy to inhaled corticosteroids for chronic asthma | Cochrane \(2005\) | LTRA and LABA agents may both be considered as add-on therapy to inhaled corticosteroids.""}")

Bash(curl -s -X POST https://metalens-ai.vercel.app/api/synthesize -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "{""prompt"": ""You are a medical research analyst. Synthesize these PubMed abstracts into exactly 5 key findings.\\n\\nFormat each finding as:\\n**N. Short Title** — One concise conclusion sentence with specific data. Cite relevant PMIDs.\\n\\nCover: 1\) main comparison, 2\) statistics, 3\) recommendation, 4\) exceptions, 5\) limitations.\\n\\nRules: base claims ONLY on abstracts, cite PMIDs, use hedging language.\\n\\n--- ABSTRACTS ---\\n\\nPMID: 35133415 | Tirzepatide vs Placebo Added to Titrated Insulin Glargine | JAMA \(2022\) | Effects of tirzepatide as addition to insulin glargine for type 2 diabetes treatment.\\n\\nPMID: 18784090 | 10-year follow-up of intensive glucose control in type 2 diabetes | NEJM \(2008\) | UKPDS patients with intensive glucose therapy had lower risk of microvascular complications.\\n\\nPMID: 38772405 | Post-trial monitoring UKPDS 91 | Lancet \(2024\) | 24-year follow-up showed major clinical benefits for intensive glycaemic control with sulfonylurea insulin or metformin therapy.\\n\\nPMID: 35965421 | Anti-hyperglycemic effects of propolis or metformin in type 2 diabetes | IJVNR \(2023\) | Evaluating propolis or metformin versus placebo on glycemic control in treatment-naive T2DM patients.\\n\\nPMID: 39406449 | Intense simplified strategy for newly diagnosed type 2 diabetes | BMJ \(2024\) | Short term intensive insulin therapy followed by oral antihyperglycaemic regimens could improve long term glycaemic outcomes.""}")

Bash(curl -s -X POST https://metalens-ai.vercel.app/api/synthesize -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "{""prompt"": ""You are a medical research analyst. Synthesize these PubMed abstracts into exactly 5 key findings.\\n\\nFormat each finding as:\\n**N. Short Title** — One concise conclusion sentence with specific data. Cite relevant PMIDs.\\n\\nCover: 1\) main comparison, 2\) statistics, 3\) recommendation, 4\) exceptions, 5\) limitations.\\n\\nRules: base claims ONLY on abstracts, cite PMIDs, use hedging language.\\n\\n--- ABSTRACTS ---\\n\\nPMID: 33125495 | Comparison of Acetaminophen with Ibuprofen for Treatment of Fever or Pain in Children Under 2 | JAMA Network Open \(2021\) | Acetaminophen and ibuprofen are the most widely prescribed OTC medications for fever and pain in children.\\n\\nPMID: 19949916 | Ibuprofen pharmacology efficacy and safety | Inflammopharmacology \(2010\) | Review of clinical uses safety and pharmacological properties of ibuprofen.\\n\\nPMID: 15184213 | Efficacy and safety of acetaminophen vs ibuprofen for children pain or fever meta-analysis | Arch Pediatr Adolesc Med \(2004\) | Summarize studies testing efficacy and safety of single-dose acetaminophen and ibuprofen for children pain or fever.\\n\\nPMID: 36945023 | Paracetamol and ibuprofen combination for acute mild-to-moderate pain in children | Ital J Pediatr \(2023\) | Acute pain is common in children. Despite guidelines pain often remains undertreated.\\n\\nPMID: 20150507 | Efficacy and safety of ibuprofen and acetaminophen meta-analysis | Ann Pharmacother \(2010\) | Evaluate analgesic and antipyretic efficacy and safety of ibuprofen compared to acetaminophen in children and adults.""}")

Bash(curl -s -X POST https://metalens-ai.vercel.app/api/synthesize -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{""prompt"": ""Summarize in 3 sentences: Aspirin is used for cardiovascular prevention. Studies show mixed results in primary prevention.""}')
